title: Ship Rotation, Thrust, Inertia, Drag, and Speed Limits · Language Clinic: Inertia Drag
summary: Thrust teleports position, diagonal motion is faster, velocity aliases facing, turning changes old momentum, drag depends on frames, and speed caps each component independently. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the inertia drag increment in frame-rate-independent inertial ship controller. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theor…
objectives: Preserve momentum and apply a time-scaled damping model whose behavior is tested across frame rates.
Integrate acceleration into velocity and velocity into position with explicit seconds and bounded time.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-ship-motion-physics-3
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-ship-motion-physics-3.
# Case 5798: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def worked_asteroids_ship_motion_physics_3(speed: float = 140.0, thrust: float = 80.0, drag: float = 0.15, max_speed: float = 260.0, dt: float = 0.016) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "playerworked3",
        "seed5798",
        "input3dekrow",
        "frameworked35798",
        "state5798worked3",
        "playtest3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if min(speed, thrust, drag, max_speed, dt) < 0.0:
        return False, speed, "motion-domain"
    accelerated = speed + thrust * dt
    damped = accelerated * max(0.0, 1.0 - drag * dt)
    bounded = min(damped, max_speed)
    if bounded > max_speed:
        return False, bounded, "speed-cap"
    assert 0.0 <= bounded <= max_speed
    return True, round(bounded, 3), "inertial-ship"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Inertia Drag evidence · Theory Inertia Drag
steps.instruction:

Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-inertia-drag-161mtw
# Competency: asteroids-inertia-drag.
# Case 4697: a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def evidence_asteroids_inertia_drag_161mtw(speed: float = 140.0, thrust: float = 80.0, drag: float = 0.15, max_speed: float = 260.0, dt: float = 0.016) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_161mtw = "161mtw-wtm161-4697"
    assert evidence_variant_161mtw
    evidence_axes_161mtw = (
        "player161mtw",
        "seed4697",
        "inputwtm161",
        "frame161mtw4697",
        "state4697161mtw",
        "playtestwtm1614697",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_161mtw)) == 6
    if min(speed, thrust, drag, max_speed, dt) < 0.0:
        return False, speed, "motion-domain"
    accelerated = speed + thrust * dt
    damped = accelerated * max(0.0, 1.0 - drag * dt)
    bounded = min(damped, max_speed)
    if bounded > max_speed:
        return False, bounded, "speed-cap"
    assert 0.0 <= bounded <= max_speed
    return True, round(bounded, 3), "inertial-ship"

Build deterministic Python evidence to integrate rotation, thrust, inertia, drag, and speed limits with frame-rate-independent Vector2 motion; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Preserve momentum and apply a time-scaled damping model whose behavior is tested across frame rates.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Inertia Drag model · Theory Inertia Drag
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Inertia Drag: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 8168 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to integrate rotation, thrust, inertia, drag, and speed limits with frame-rate-independent Vector2 motion; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Preserve momentum and apply a time-scaled damping model whose behavior is tested across frame rates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Inertia Drag model · Theory Inertia Drag.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Inertia Drag model · Theory Inertia Drag, keep Inertia Drag connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Preserve momentum and apply a time-scaled damping model whose behavior is tested across frame rates.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: asteroids-inertia-drag-161mtw(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_asteroids_inertia_drag_161mtw\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?min\(speed)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?thrust \* dt)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?1.0 - drag \* dt)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?min\(damped, max_speed)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
